original: Macarius teaches that the work is to accuse oneself rather than another.
english: The judge inside becomes useful only when it turns homeward.
spanish: El juez interior sólo se vuelve útil cuando se vuelve hacia casa.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Macarius 1
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Macarius calls the brothers to weep before the place where tears will burn.
english: The tear chosen now may spare the tear forced later.
spanish: La lágrima elegida ahora puede ahorrar la lágrima forzada después.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Macarius 12
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Macarius points to prayer that continues beneath every task.
english: Prayer becomes ceaseless when it stops waiting for empty time.
spanish: La oración se vuelve incesante cuando deja de esperar tiempo vacío.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Macarius 19
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Macarius answers a request for a word by saying: let us weep.
english: Some words do not explain the way; they open the eyes.
spanish: Algunas palabras no explican el camino; abren los ojos.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Macarius 34
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Moses warns that judging another is a trap for the one who judges.
english: Judgment leaves the cell disguised as concern and returns as blindness.
spanish: El juicio sale de la celda disfrazado de cuidado y vuelve como ceguera.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Moses 2
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Moses counsels bearing with one's neighbor rather than measuring him.
english: The neighbor is not a problem to solve but a weight to carry with mercy.
spanish: El prójimo no es un problema que resolver sino un peso que cargar con misericordia.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Moses 3
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Moses points to humility as the protection of the monk.
english: The low place is not defeat; it is shelter from the fall.
spanish: El lugar bajo no es derrota; es refugio contra la caída.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Moses 4
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Moses speaks of guarding peace before every other work.
english: A work done without peace spends more than it earns.
spanish: Una obra hecha sin paz gasta más de lo que gana.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Moses 6
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Poemen returns a long way to ask the thought he remembered at the door.
english: The unfinished question is worth more than the opened door.
spanish: La pregunta inconclusa vale más que la puerta abierta.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Poemen 1
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Poemen turns the work inward, toward silence and watchfulness.
english: Silence is not absence; it is attention with fewer exits.
spanish: El silencio no es ausencia; es atención con menos salidas.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Poemen 3
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Poemen treats self-justification as a loss of the desert way.
english: The self defended too quickly has not yet heard the wound.
spanish: El yo defendido demasiado pronto aún no ha oído la herida.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Poemen 8
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Poemen teaches patience as the daily shape of endurance.
english: Patience is not waiting empty; it is staying present without revenge.
spanish: La paciencia no es esperar vacío; es permanecer presente sin venganza.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Poemen 13
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Poemen speaks of the heart pierced enough to turn.
english: Compunction is the wound that opens back toward life.
spanish: La compunción es la herida que se abre de nuevo hacia la vida.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Poemen 26
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Poemen measures progress by what becomes smaller in the self.
english: Progress appears as less noise around the same obedience.
spanish: El progreso aparece como menos ruido alrededor de la misma obediencia.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Poemen 35
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Poemen says the work is to watch oneself and judge no one.
english: The eye that watches itself has little time left to condemn.
spanish: El ojo que se vigila a sí mismo tiene poco tiempo para condenar.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Poemen 64
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Poemen locates charity in the concrete care of the brother.
english: Love becomes real where it costs time, attention, and bread.
spanish: El amor se vuelve real donde cuesta tiempo, atención y pan.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Poemen 116
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Syncletica speaks of the inner fire that begins repentance.
english: The heart is softened by a fire that does not consume it.
spanish: El corazón se ablanda con un fuego que no lo consume.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Syncletica 1
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Syncletica teaches control as the guarding of desire before it grows.
english: A desire is easiest to guide before it learns its own road.
spanish: Un deseo es más fácil de guiar antes de que aprenda su propio camino.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Syncletica 4
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Syncletica warns that visions must be tested by humility.
english: A light that resists humility is not yet safe to follow.
spanish: Una luz que resiste la humildad aún no es segura para seguir.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Syncletica 18
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Syncletica returns spiritual victory to humility.
english: The highest ascent keeps the face near the ground.
spanish: El ascenso más alto mantiene el rostro cerca del suelo.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Syncletica 26
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

artifact: Sarah 3 — Not Looking at the River
original: Sarah lives beside a river for sixty years and does not lift her eyes to look at it.
english: Renunciation can be measured by what remains near and unused.
spanish: La renuncia puede medirse por lo que permanece cerca y sin usar.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Sarah 3
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Sarah answers visitors who try to humiliate her with steady discernment.
english: The insult loses force when the one insulted has nothing to prove.
spanish: El insulto pierde fuerza cuando quien lo recibe no tiene nada que probar.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Sarah 4
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Sisoes speaks of bearing with the neighbor as part of the work.
english: The other person's difficulty is one of the tools of the cell.
spanish: La dificultad de la otra persona es una de las herramientas de la celda.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Sisoes 1
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Sisoes returns the monk to humility as the safe place.
english: The safest height in the desert is the ground.
spanish: La altura más segura en el desierto es el suelo.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Sisoes 9
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Silvanus teaches vigilance as a guard over the heart.
english: The gate of the heart is watched before enemies are named.
spanish: La puerta del corazón se vigila antes de nombrar enemigos.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Silvanus 4
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.

original: Hyperchius compares the experienced monk to a lion facing wild desires.
english: Experience makes the inner beast afraid to approach.
spanish: La experiencia hace que la bestia interior tema acercarse.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Hyperchius 1
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF. Short working rendering.
